description: Entry-level salary benchmarks for Hong Kong graduates by industry sector, based on CTgoodjobs 2025 Graduate Salary Survey, HKBU GBA Pay Survey, and JIJIS data. All figures in HKD/month.
source: CTgoodjobs 2025 Graduate Salary & Employment Survey, HKBU Greater Bay Area Pay and Benefits Survey 2025, JIJIS (Joint University Job Information System), HK Government Census & Statistics Department

overallAverage.note: Starting salaries plateaued at ~HK$19,500 in 2025, ending 5 years of consecutive growth. Expected 2.8% increase in 2026.

sectors.note: Highest-paying sector for fresh graduates in 2025. Driven by Northern Metropolis, public housing pipeline, and infrastructure projects.
Civil service salaries moderated in 2025. Still competitive with strong job security. Includes statutory bodies and public utilities.
AI, fintech, and digital transformation driving demand. One of few sectors showing growth. Higher end for AI/ML and specialized roles.
Oversupply of graduates has reduced average entry salaries, but elite roles (IB, PE, trading) still command premiums. Fintech roles growing.
Consistent demand from HK's aging population. Clinical roles command higher premiums. Health informatics emerging.
Big 4 audit and consulting hire large volumes. MBB consulting at higher end. ESG advisory growing.
One of the lower-paying entry sectors but strong career growth potential. Digital marketing and e-commerce roles pay better than traditional marketing.
HK's role as regional logistics hub supports demand. E-commerce logistics and supply chain analytics growing.

educationLevelMultiplier.note: Approximate multipliers relative to bachelor's degree. Actual varies by sector and employer.

universityTierAdjustment.note: HKU, CUHK, HKUST graduates command slight premium in certain sectors (especially finance and consulting). Effect is minimal after 2-3 years of experience.
